001package guiFirstAdmin;
002
003import javafx.geometry.Pos;
004import javafx.scene.control.Alert;
005import javafx.scene.control.Button;
006import javafx.scene.control.Label;
007import javafx.scene.control.PasswordField;
008import javafx.scene.control.TextField;
009import javafx.scene.control.Alert.AlertType;
010import javafx.scene.layout.Pane;
011import javafx.scene.text.Font;
012import javafx.stage.Stage;
013import javafx.scene.Scene;
014
015
016/*******
017 * <p> Title: ViewFirstAdmin Class</p>
018 * 
019 * <p> Description: The FirstAdmin Page View.  This class is used to require the very first user of
020 * the system to specify an Admin Username and Password when there is no database for the
021 * application.  This avoids the common weakness practice of hard coding credentials into the 
022 * application.</p>
023 * 
024 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
025 * 
026 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
027 * 
028 * @version 1.00                2025-08-15 Initial version
029 *  
030 */
031
032public class ViewFirstAdmin {
033
034        /*-********************************************************************************************
035
036        Attributes
037
038         */
039
040        // These are the application values required by the user interface
041        
042        private static double width = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_WIDTH;
043        private static double height = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_HEIGHT;
044
045        // These are the widget attributes for the GUI
046        
047        // The GUI informs the user about the purpose of this page, provides three text inputs fields
048        // for the user to specify a username for this account and two copies of the password to be
049        // used (they must match), a button to request that the account be established, and a quit
050        // but to abort the action and stop the application.
051        private static Label label_ApplicationTitle = new Label("Foundation Application (TP1)");
052        private static Label label_TitleLine1 = 
053                        new Label(" You are the first user.  You must be an administrator.");
054        
055        private static Label label_TitleLine2 = 
056                        new Label("Enter the Admin's Username, the Password twice, and then click on " + 
057                                        "Setup Admin Account.");
058        
059        protected static Label label_PasswordsDoNotMatch = new Label();
060        protected static TextField text_AdminUsername = new TextField();
061        protected static PasswordField text_AdminPassword1 = new PasswordField();
062        protected static PasswordField text_AdminPassword2 = new PasswordField();
063        private static Button button_AdminSetup = new Button("Setup Admin Account");
064
065        // This alert is used should the user enter two passwords that do not match
066        protected static Alert alertUsernamePasswordError = new Alert(AlertType.INFORMATION);
067
068        // This button allow the user to abort creating the first admin account and terminate
069        private static Button button_Quit = new Button("Quit");
070
071        // These attributes are used to configure the page and populate it with this user's information
072        protected static Stage theStage;        
073        private static Pane theRootPane;
074        private static Scene theFirstAdminScene = null;
075        private static final int theRole = 1;           // Admin: 1; Role1: 2; Role2: 3
076                
077        
078        /*-********************************************************************************************
079
080        Constructor
081
082         */
083        
084        /**********
085         * <p> Method: public displayFirstAdmin(Stage ps) </p>
086         * 
087         * <p> Description: This method is called when the application first starts. It create an
088         * an instance of the View class.  
089         * 
090         * NOTE: As described below, this code does not implement MVC using the singleton pattern used
091         * by most of the pages as the code is written this way because we know with certainty that it
092         * will only be called once.  For this reason, we directly call the private class constructor.
093         * 
094         * @param ps specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods
095         */
096        public static void displayFirstAdmin(Stage ps) {
097                
098                // Establish the references to the GUI.  There is no user yet.
099                theStage = ps;                  // Establish a reference to the JavaFX Stage
100                
101                // This page is only called once so there is no need to save the reference to it
102                new ViewFirstAdmin();   // Create an instance of the class
103                
104                // Populate the dynamic aspects of the GUI with the data from the user and the current
105                // state of the system.
106                applicationMain.FoundationsMain.activeHomePage = theRole;       // 1: Admin; 2: Role1; 3 Roles2
107
108                // Set the title for the window, display the page, and wait for the Admin to do something
109                theStage.setTitle("CSE 360 Foundation Code: First User Account Setup"); 
110                theStage.setScene(theFirstAdminScene);
111                theStage.show();
112        }
113
114        /**********
115         * <p> Method: private ViewFirstAdmin() </p>
116         * 
117         * <p> Description: This constructor is called when the application first starts. It must 
118         * handle when no user has been established.  It is never called again, either during the first
119         * run of the program after reboot or during normal operations of the program.  (This page is
120         * only used when there is no database for the application or there is a database, but there
121         * no users in the database.
122         * 
123         * If there is no database or there is a database, but there are no users, it means that the
124         * person starting the system is an administrator, so a special GUI is provided to allow this
125         * Admin to create an account by setting a username and password.
126         * 
127         * If there is at least one user, this page is not called.  Since this is only used one when
128         * the system is being set up, this MVC code does not use a singleton protocol.  For this
129         * reason, do not use this as a typical MVC pattern.</p>
130         * 
131         * This is the View of the Model, View, Controller architectural pattern.  Due to how it is
132         * called, it does not follow the usual singleton implementation pattern as explained above.
133         * 
134         * The view sets up the window and all of the Graphical User Interface (GUI) widgets.  Once
135         * they are all set, the window is displayed to the user, the View returns to the starting main
136         * method, and that execution thread ends.  The JavaFX thread continues to "execute" waiting
137         * for the user to interact with the GUI.  Based on which widget is used, changes to the
138         * display are made and/or a Controller method is called to perform some action.
139         * 
140         */
141        private ViewFirstAdmin() {
142
143                // Create the Pane for the list of widgets and the Scene for the window
144                theRootPane = new Pane();
145                theFirstAdminScene = new Scene(theRootPane, width, height);
146
147                // Label theScene with the name of the system startup screen
148                setupLabelUI(label_ApplicationTitle, "Arial", 32, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 10);
149
150                // Label to display the welcome message for the first user
151                setupLabelUI(label_TitleLine1, "Arial", 24, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 70);
152
153                // Label to display the welcome message for the first user
154                setupLabelUI(label_TitleLine2, "Arial", 18, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 130);
155
156                // Establish the text input operand field for the Admin username
157                setupTextUI(text_AdminUsername, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 50, 160, 
158                                true);
159                text_AdminUsername.setPromptText("Enter Admin Username");
160                text_AdminUsername.textProperty().addListener((_, _, _) 
161                                -> {ControllerFirstAdmin.setAdminUsername(); });
162
163                // Establish the text input operand field for the password
164                setupTextUI(text_AdminPassword1, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 50, 210, 
165                                true);
166                text_AdminPassword1.setPromptText("Enter Admin Password");
167                text_AdminPassword1.textProperty().addListener((_, _, _)
168                                -> {ControllerFirstAdmin.setAdminPassword1(); });
169
170                // Establish the text input operand field for the password
171                setupTextUI(text_AdminPassword2, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 50, 260, 
172                                true);
173                text_AdminPassword2.setPromptText("Enter Admin Password Again");
174                text_AdminPassword2.textProperty().addListener((_, _, _) 
175                                -> {ControllerFirstAdmin.setAdminPassword2(); });
176
177                // Set up the Log In button
178                setupButtonUI(button_AdminSetup, "Dialog", 18, 200, Pos.CENTER, 475, 210);
179                button_AdminSetup.setOnAction((_) -> {
180                        ControllerFirstAdmin.doSetupAdmin(theStage,1); 
181                        });
182
183                // Label to display the Passwords do not match error message
184                setupLabelUI(label_PasswordsDoNotMatch, "Arial", 18, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 300);
185
186                setupButtonUI(button_Quit, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 300, 520);
187                button_Quit.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerFirstAdmin.performQuit(); });
188
189                // Place all of the just-initialized GUI elements into the pane
190                theRootPane.getChildren().addAll(label_ApplicationTitle, label_TitleLine1,
191                                label_TitleLine2, text_AdminUsername, text_AdminPassword1, 
192                                text_AdminPassword2, button_AdminSetup,
193                                label_PasswordsDoNotMatch, button_Quit);
194        }
195        
196        
197        /*-********************************************************************************************
198
199        Helper methods to reduce code length
200
201         */
202        
203        /**********
204         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a label
205         * 
206         * @param l             The Label object to be initialized
207         * @param ff    The font to be used
208         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
209         * @param w             The width of the Label
210         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
211         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
212         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
213         */
214
215        private void setupLabelUI(Label l, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
216                l.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
217                l.setMinWidth(w);
218                l.setAlignment(p);
219                l.setLayoutX(x);
220                l.setLayoutY(y);                
221        }
222
223
224        /**********
225         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a button
226         * 
227         * @param b             The Button object to be initialized
228         * @param ff    The font to be used
229         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
230         * @param w             The width of the Button
231         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
232         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
233         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
234         */
235        private void setupButtonUI(Button b, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
236                b.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
237                b.setMinWidth(w);
238                b.setAlignment(p);
239                b.setLayoutX(x);
240                b.setLayoutY(y);                
241        }
242
243        
244        /**********
245         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a text field
246         * 
247         * @param t             The TextField object to be initialized
248         * @param ff    The font to be used
249         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
250         * @param w             The width of the Button
251         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
252         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
253         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
254         * @param e             The flag (Boolean) that specifies if this field is editable
255         */
256        private void setupTextUI(TextField t, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y, 
257                        boolean e){
258                t.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
259                t.setMinWidth(w);
260                t.setMaxWidth(w);
261                t.setAlignment(p);
262                t.setLayoutX(x);
263                t.setLayoutY(y);                
264                t.setEditable(e);
265        }       
266}
